title: Lancashire Hotpot

overview.description: A traditional Lancashire casserole of slow-cooked lamb, onions and rich stock, topped with crisp, golden slices of potato. Simple ingredients transformed into classic British comfort food.

ingredients: 1kg lamb shoulder, cut into large chunks
2 tsp fresh thyme leaves (or 1 tsp dried)
1.5kg floury potatoes, peeled and thinly sliced
method: Heat the vegetable oil in a large casserole dish over a medium-high heat.
Season the lamb with salt and pepper, dust lightly with the flour and brown in batches. Remove and set aside.
Reduce the heat and cook the onions for 10–15 minutes until softened and lightly coloured.
Add the carrots and cook for 5 minutes.
Stir in the garlic and cook for 1 minute.
Return the lamb to the casserole and add the stock, Worcestershire sauce, bay leaves and thyme.
Arrange the sliced potatoes over the top, slightly overlapping them to completely cover the filling.
Brush the potatoes generously with the melted butter.
Cover with a lid and bake at 160°C fan (180°C conventional) for 2 hours.
Remove the lid, brush the potatoes with any remaining butter if needed and return to the oven for a further 30–40 minutes until the potatoes are crisp and deeply golden.
Leave to rest for 10 minutes before serving.

learning.did-you-know: Lancashire Hotpot dates back to the Industrial Revolution, when workers needed hearty meals that could cook slowly while they were at work.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How to brown meat for maximum flavour, layer potatoes for an attractive finish and use slow cooking to tenderise lamb shoulder.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: A wonderful winter meal that's economical, deeply satisfying and perfect for slow Saturday cooking.
chapman-tips: Lamb shoulder becomes beautifully tender after slow cooking and is worth choosing over leaner cuts.
Take the time to overlap the potato slices neatly—they form both the lid and the presentation.
Leaving the lid off for the final stage creates the classic crisp potato topping.
A splash of homemade lamb stock takes this dish to another level if you have some in the freezer.
notes.batch-cooking: Freeze cooled portions for up to three months. Defrost overnight before reheating thoroughly.
notes.leftovers: Excellent reheated in the oven, where the potato topping crisps up again.
